descript.abstract: World Latitude and Longitude Grid, 2022 is a line theme that represents a 1- by 1-degree latitude-longitude grid covering the world with attributes that allow it to display grids at intervals of 1, 5, 10, 15, 20, and 30 degrees. To display a grid with a 1-degree interval, display all of the lines. To display a coarser grid (for example, a 15-degree interval), in the Symbology pane in ArcGIS Pro or in the Layer Properties dialog box under the Symbology tab in ArcMap, choose the DEGREE15 field and value Y.
descript.purpose: World Latitude and Longitude Grids provides latitude and longitude lines for use as an overlay for world-level maps.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States– Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing and consulting services to your local Esri Regional Office. For support, contact Esri by telephone (voice) between 5:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. Pacific time, Monday through Friday, by dialing +1-888-377-4575; facsimile (fax) available at +1-909-792-0960; or visit https://support.esri.com/; Esri holidays excluded. Email and chat are available through the Support page on My Esri (https://my.esri.com).
Outside the United States– Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing, sales, support, and consulting services to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0. For other questions or comments, you may contact Esri headquarters by email, telephone, or fax or write to us.

dataqual.logic: The dataset is converted to file geodatabase (FGDB) format using tools in ArcGIS. The geometry is checked, and if needed repaired.
dataqual.complete: After processing, the dataset is checked for drawing display and number of records and file sizes compared with source materials.

horizpa.horizpar: The geospatial part of this data set was mathematically generated using the ArcInfo Generate tool. In this manner, the locations were computed. The positional accuracy is limited only by the resolution of the data set, which is less than a meter.

procstep.procdesc: The following steps were performed by Esri: Created the 1- by 1-degree grid data set using the ArcInfo Generate tool. Used projection, coordinate, and attribute information from Esri Data & Maps 10 World Latitude and Longitude Grids data set. Added and coded the attributes VALUE, DEGREE5, DEGREE10, DEGREE15, DEGREE20, and DEGREE30. Converted the data set to SDC. Copied the projection file (.prj), and created ArcGIS layer file (.lyr) and spatial indices.
Converted the dataset into a file geodatabase.

altsys.altenc: Explicit elevation coordinate included with horizontal coordinates

enttyp.enttypd: The lines represent a 1- by 1-degree latitude–longitude grid for the world.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The degree value and the latitude or longitude directional reference of the grid line.
The indicator whether a grid line is part of the 5 by 5 degree grid.

edom.edomvd: The grid line is part of the 5 by 5 degree grid.
The grid line is not part of the 5 by 5 degree grid.

attr.attrdef: The indicator whether a grid line is part of the 10 by 10 degree grid.

edom.edomvd: The grid line is part of the 10 by 10 degree grid.
The grid line is not part of the 10 by 10 degree grid.

attr.attrdef: The indicator whether a grid line is part of the 15 by 15 degree grid.

edom.edomvd: The grid line is part of the 15 by 15 degree grid.
The grid line is not part of the 15 by 15 degree grid.

attr.attrdef: The indicator whether a grid line is part of the 20 by 20 degree grid.

edom.edomvd: The grid line is part of the 20 by 20 degree grid.
The grid line is not part of the 20 by 20 degree grid.

attr.attrdef: The indicator whether a grid line is part of the 30 by 30 degree grid.

edom.edomvd: The grid line is part of the 30 by 30 degree grid.
The grid line is not part of the 30 by 30 degree grid.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States, contact the Esri Sales staff at +1-800-447-9778 or visit https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=1 for more information about our software and data.
Outside the United States, direct all inquiries to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0.

distinfo.distliab: See access and use constraints information.

digtinfo.formcont: To use this data requires software that works with FGDB files.

stdorder.ordering: Data and Maps is available only as part of ArcGIS software.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
